rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed the for-loop that uses destLen = strlen(dest) as the bound and explained that copying dest[i] = data[i] over destLen iterations overreads the smaller 50-byte source buffer. This directly describes the over-read mechanism at the bad variant’s flaw site.
rationale.local_fix: They proposed changing the loop to use the source length (e.g., strlen(data) or a strnlen-limited value), which bounds reads to the source buffer’s size. This satisfies the requirement to bound the read by the buffer length.
rationale.global_fix: They recommended passing explicit source lengths through the API and using bounded operations (strnlen/strncpy), avoiding reliance on NUL-termination. This matches the requirement to pass explicit lengths instead of sentinel-terminated buffers.
